author: Dr. Lisa Tran, MD (ID fellow) / Dr. Hassan Nasser, MD (ID attending)
content:
INFECTIOUS DISEASE CONSULTATION — MICU
Date/Time: 2/19/2027, 11:00 AM (Hospital Day 5)
Patient: Nguyen, Lan T (MRN-90215888)
Consulting team: MICU + Pulmonary (Dr. Chen)
Interpreter: Patient remains intubated and sedated. Vietnamese phone interpreter present for family update (son Quang).

REASON FOR CONSULT:
(1) Antibiotic optimization for biliary sepsis in a patient with non-anaphylactic PCN allergy (childhood rash); (2) rule out active tuberculosis given documented latent TB infection (2010), Vietnamese origin, and newly identified 2.5 cm right upper lobe pulmonary nodule.

BRIEF HOSPITAL COURSE TO DATE:
72-year-old Vietnamese-speaking woman with severe biliary pancreatitis status post emergent ERCP with sphincterotomy and stone extraction (2/16) for choledocholithiasis with biliary sepsis. Complicated by moderate ARDS requiring mechanical ventilation (since 2/17), septic shock (now off vasopressors as of this AM), and AKI on CKD (Cr peaked at 2.6, now 2.2). Currently on pip/tazo + vancomycin pending culture data.

Bacteriologic data:
- Admission blood cultures (2/15): 1/2 coag-negative staph; speciation in progress, likely contaminant.
- Repeat blood cultures (2/17): No growth at 48 hours; will follow.
- Biliary fluid culture (intra-procedural ERCP 2/16): Gram-negative rods on Gram stain; culture growing E. coli (susceptibility report pending; preliminary suggests susceptible to ceftriaxone, ciprofloxacin, pip/tazo).
- Urine culture: No growth.
- Sputum gram stain (today, via ET tube suction): Mixed Gram-positive cocci and Gram-negative rods, few WBCs; routine culture pending.

TB-RELEVANT HISTORY:
- Vietnamese-born (Hà Nam Province, rural), immigrated 1990 at age 36.
- BCG vaccinated as a child (~1956 mass campaign, visible scar L deltoid).
- Positive PPD 14 mm + positive QuantiFERON 2010 (per Dr. O'Sullivan, Hopkins Bayview Pulm/ID).
- CXR 2010 (in-office) — normal.
- Sputum AFB smears 2010 — negative x 3.
- Sputum AFB cultures 2010 — negative (final 8 weeks).
- INH treatment for latent TB recommended but DECLINED by patient — repeatedly declined over 16 years per PCP notes (8 documented declinations).
- No prior symptoms suggestive of active TB.
- No travel back to Vietnam since 2014.
- No known TB contacts in family.
- Brother d. 3/2026 of squamous cell lung cancer in Vietnam (heavy smoker) — not TB-related per family.

NEWLY IDENTIFIED FINDING:
2.5 cm right upper lobe pulmonary nodule on serial CXRs (2/16, 2/17, 2/18). Pulmonary recommendation for dedicated chest CT and TB rule-out.

IMPRESSION:

#1 BILIARY SEPSIS (E. coli, biliary source) — improving clinically.

#2 NEED TO RULE OUT ACTIVE PULMONARY TUBERCULOSIS
Given documented LTBI, Vietnamese origin (TB-endemic), 2.5 cm pulmonary nodule, and now the context of acute illness with possible (though clinically unsupported) reactivation. Active TB has been described as a cause of incidental pulmonary nodules, particularly in foreign-born patients from high-burden regions.

#3 ANTIBIOTIC OPTIMIZATION
Penicillin allergy: childhood rash, non-anaphylactic, never re-challenged. She has tolerated pip/tazo so far x 4 days without reaction — confirms a low cross-reactivity risk for this β-lactam. We will continue pip/tazo.

RECOMMENDATIONS:

A) TB RULE-OUT WORKUP:
1. Place patient in airborne isolation pending workup — single negative-pressure room (currently in negative-pressure-capable MICU room, confirmed today).
2. Collect 3 sputum samples for AFB smear (via ET tube suction, ≥8 hours apart). All staff to wear N95 respirators.
3. Send 1 sputum sample for nucleic acid amplification testing (NAAT, Xpert MTB/RIF) — turnaround ~24 hours.
4. Send 3 sputum AFB cultures (turnaround up to 8 weeks).
5. Consider repeat IGRA (positive at baseline; not particularly helpful for active vs latent distinction but a baseline trend).
6. Once 2 AFB smears + NAAT are negative, airborne isolation can be discontinued. The remaining smear and cultures should still be processed.

B) ANTIBIOTICS:
1. Continue pip/tazo 3.375 g IV q6h (renally adjusted; currently dose appropriate for Cr 2.2).
2. Discontinue vancomycin — admission blood culture CoNS confirmed contaminant; no MRSA risk indicators; biliary culture E. coli only.
3. Plan total 10-14 days of antibiotic therapy from source control (ERCP date 2/16); will reassess clinical status and rate of WBC normalization. Anticipate end-date ~2/26 to 3/2 with step-down to PO if possible (ciprofloxacin reasonable target if susceptible).

C) IF AFB POSITIVE:
Unlikely but if any smear or NAAT positive, will initiate empiric 4-drug RIPE therapy and re-engage Maryland Department of Health for case management. Document airborne isolation precautions to family in Vietnamese.

D) PULMONARY NODULE WORKUP:
Deferred to outpatient management — coordinated with pulmonary consult. Once she is off the ventilator and stable enough to transport, dedicated chest CT (with appropriate contrast pre-medication for her iodinated-contrast allergy if contrast is desired) should be obtained. Many institutions have pulmonary nodule clinics with structured follow-up; recommend referral to one.

E) FAMILY DISCUSSION (with son Quang, via Vietnamese interpreter, ~ 30 minutes):
- Reviewed the rationale for TB testing in light of LTBI history and the newly identified lung nodule.
- Explained that active TB is unlikely given absence of symptoms (no cough at baseline, no hemoptysis, no chronic weight loss), normal 2010 CXR, and lack of recent TB exposure — but it must be ruled out.
- Explained that the nodule itself raises questions that go beyond TB and will need its own outpatient workup. We did not promise a specific etiology.
- Family understood. They are aware of airborne isolation precautions including N95 use for visitors.

Follow-up: Will check AFB smear results daily; expect 1st smear back tomorrow AM (2/20). Plan to discontinue airborne isolation once 2 smears + NAAT negative, anticipated 2/22 if smears come back as expected. Will continue to round on antibiotic management daily until discharge or until ID involvement no longer needed.
attestation:
Electronically signed by:
Dr. Lisa Tran, MD (ID fellow)
Dr. Hassan Nasser, MD (ID attending)
Date: 2/19/2027
